label: Iran Killed
source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera
contestNote: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed including 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA: 1,407+ civilian deaths including 214 children. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed, 18,551+ injured, 300+ health facilities damaged. Iran's official floor: 1,500+ killed. WHO: 18 hospitals struck. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 310+ hrs) severely limits verification. Day 27: Iran rejects Trump's 15-point plan; anti-war protests continue in Tehran, Isfahan, Tabriz.

label: US Killed
deltaNote: 13 KIA confirmed: 6 Army Reserve killed in Kuwait drone strike (Feb 28), Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (died Mar 8 from wounds), 6 KC-135 crew (names released by Pentagon — crash Mar 13 western Iraq, cause under investigation). ~140 wounded total. USS Gerald Ford fire (Mar 18) — no sailor deaths confirmed. Joe Kent (NCTC) resigned Day 19. 82nd Airborne elements (~1,000+) now deploying to CENTCOM AOR.

label: Israel Killed
source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 19+ killed in Israel total. 4,800+ wounded. Mar 27 (Day 28): 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rocket fire; IDF Chief Zamir warns of 'severe strain' — 15,000-soldier manpower shortfall across five fronts. Mar 24: Iranian missile landed in Tel Aviv — 3 injured. Mar 18: Elderly couple (70s) killed in Ramat Gan. 175+ Iranian attack waves since Feb 28.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: 22+ total across Gulf states. Mar 24: Iran struck fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking fire; Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly 'losing patience' and may join strikes on Iran (Bloomberg). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (suspended operations). Qatar LNG remains halted Day 27. Saudi Arabia intercepted 37+ drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
deltaNote: 1,094+ killed including 130+ children; 1.2M+ displaced. Mar 25: Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets at IDF positions near Naqura and Qawzah; IDF struck Beirut cruise missile facilities and bridges. Mar 24: Israeli airstrike on Bchamoun apartment block killed 3 including 3-year-old girl. IDF declared 30km security zone to Litani River. ~700 Hezbollah fighters eliminated per IDF (since operations began).

label: Brent Crude
deltaNote: Brent ~$109/bbl Day 28; slight dip as Trump extended Hormuz deadline again to April 6, citing 10 Pakistan-flagged tankers allowed through as goodwill gesture. Iran selectively allowing China/India/Pakistan/Russia-flagged vessels through Hormuz under coordination. Next hard deadline: April 6 at 8 PM ET for full reopening. IEA: 'greatest global energy security challenge in history.' IEA released 400M bbl from reserves. US lifted 30-day sanctions on Iranian oil aboard tankers (unload by Apr 19).

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Qatar LNG offline (Day 2) — global LNG and fertilizer supply chains disrupted. Hormuz selective passage: Iran allowing China/India/Pakistan-flagged vessels under coordination. 20M bbl/day backed up; 5M diverted to alternate routes. UN FAO emergency alert on food price spike. European natural gas TTF +22% cumulative since war onset. Slovenia: first EU fuel rationing. Spain: €5B emergency energy aid.

label: Countries Affected
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / France 24 / CNN / NewsNation
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France, Switzerland, Pakistan, Slovenia. Day 28: Trump extends Hormuz deadline to April 6 (2nd extension). Pakistan pushing for direct US-Iran talks in Islamabad. IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed in IDF strike — key architect of Hormuz blockade. IDF strikes in Kermanshah and Dezful. Iran retaliates with 2 missile barrages at central Israel.
